---
title: "Start fast and reduce friction"
---

## Outcome

By the end of this step, every attendee should be signed in, oriented, and ready to click through the first build flow without hesitation.

:::: {.visual-grid}
::: {.lesson-panel}
<span class="metric">10 min</span>
<div class="microcopy">Target duration for access, orientation, and reassurance.</div>
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
<span class="metric">1 goal</span>
<div class="microcopy">Remove uncertainty before introducing agent design.</div>
:::
::::

::: {.callout-important}
## Instructor move

Narrate the interface in terms of intent: where to create, where to ground, where to test, and where to publish.
:::

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A(Access workspace) --> B(Open Copilot Studio)
    B --> C(Scan the left navigation)
    C --> D(Find agent settings)
    D --> E(Find test chat and publish paths)
```

::: {.placeholder-figure}
![](../assets/placeholders/copilot-studio-ui.svg)
:::

## What to point out live

- where a new agent starts
- where instructions live
- where knowledge sources are attached
- where testing happens before publishing

::: {.callout-tip}
## Keep the framing light

Attendees do not need a taxonomy lecture here. They need confidence that the product is navigable.
:::
